title: centaurea sublanata
intro: Centaurea sublanata, also known as the subalpine knapweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the aster family (Asteraceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Centaurea sublanata include its ability to treat gastrointestinal problems, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, as well as its potential to alleviate respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Centaurea sublanata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
